playbook.intent: Multi-month migration (monolith→services, on-prem→cloud, legacy stack→modern) → target system live with traffic cut over, rollback proven, legacy decom plan signed, performance + cost SLAs met, AI agent leveraged but never trusted blind.
playbook.scope: End-to-end migration project led by an outsource senior. Output: target system live in production, traffic cut over with rollback proven, legacy decom plan signed, performance + cost SLAs met. AI coding agent is used heavily but every change passes human review. Reconciliation evidence retained for audit.

success-criteria.criterion: Target system live serving 100% of production traffic
Rollback drill executed at least once
Legacy decom plan signed by client sponsor
Performance + cost SLAs measured and met

stage.name: Current-state discovery
stage.intent: Inhale the legacy reality before designing the future.

tasks.task: Strategy-analysis: current state of business processes
Process mining where logs exist
Business process + data analysis
Interface analysis on every integration boundary

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the migration team can describe the legacy on a whiteboard from memory.

stage.name: Target design
stage.intent: Design the smallest target architecture that satisfies the SLAs.

tasks.task: Future-state strategy + change strategy
Architecture choices: microservices vs strangler vs lift-shift
Apply DDD aggregates / events / repositories where useful
Cross-cutting concerns: observability, reliability, anti-corruption layer
Design event-driven boundaries where they cut coupling

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the target arch is explainable in one diagram + 5 ADRs.

stage.name: Implementation plan
stage.intent: Sequence the work into independently shippable slices.

tasks.task: Write implementation plan with parallelisable slices
Insert quality gates and code-review checkpoints
Design data-pipeline strategy where applicable
Inter-service communication contracts (saga, circuit breaker)
Plan AI-agent usage envelope per slice

stage.decision-gate: Advance when slices are independently shippable AND review gates are explicit.

stage.name: Build + dual-run
stage.intent: Build slices, dual-run with the legacy, reconcile constantly.

tasks.task: Implement slices in parallel where dependencies allow
Reconciliation checks between legacy and target
Backup posture for both systems during dual-run
API monitoring (metrics + alerting) on the target
Risk-management cycle each sprint

stage.decision-gate: Advance when dual-run reconciliation is green for ≥1 full business cycle.

stage.name: Cutover + decom
stage.intent: Cut traffic, prove rollback, decom the legacy.

tasks.task: Blue/green or canary cutover (link to existing playbook)
Rollback drill before opening the floodgates
Benefits-realisation review against SLAs
Project-closure ceremony
Lessons-learned retro for the next migration

stage.decision-gate: Engagement closes when 100% of traffic runs on target, rollback drill is logged, and legacy decom date is signed.

extra.value: [Greenfield product feature work — migration only, Re-architecting the business model — technical migration, Indefinite parallel-run — decom plan is part of scope]
[Legacy system is documented enough to extract requirements, Target architecture pattern agreed at SoW level, 'Production traffic profile measurable (logs, metrics)']

[Re-architecting the business while you migrate — keeps the cutover date sliding, Trusting AI-generated migration scripts without reconciliation — silent data loss, Skipping the rollback drill because the team is exhausted — that's exactly when you need it]
['Can we roll back in <1 hour if the cutover breaks?', 'Did reconciliation prove the data, not just sample it?', 'Did we measure the SLAs we promised, or the SLAs we found convenient?']

{human: faion get-content legacy-to-modern-migration-project --format md, agent: faion get-content legacy-to-modern-migration-project --format context, json: faion get-content legacy-to-modern-migration-project --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft for F-064 P4 outsource-specialist persona, global angle'}]
